commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=afd2c3707b7379140b1f246f118dc64087c57119)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a model-specific environmental impact estimator, Eco rows in cost/provider UI, tooltip text, localization strings, methodology docs, a PNG proof image, and focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings via source inspection; no runtime app reproduction was run. The code hardcodes the disputed constants and OpenAI/Mistral projection builders still omit sessionDay while setting sessionTokens.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the main blocker is product/methodology approval plus contributor runtime proof, not a narrow code repair.

target.title: Feature/environmental methodology update
target.body:
## Status

This PR was closed by the maintainer pending product/methodology approval. The branch has been updated for a complete technical audit trail, but the PR has not been reopened.

The environmental factors are now unavailable in release builds. A preview is possible only in `DEBUG` builds with the explicit `CODEXBAR_PREVIEW_UNAPPROVED_ENVIRONMENTAL_IMPACT=1` environment variable. This prevents unapproved environmental claims from shipping while preserving a reviewable implementation.

## Changes

- Give every environmental row a stable semantic identity (`energyToday`, `co2Today`, `energyWindow`, `co2Window`) instead of deriving identity from localized display text.
- Populate `sessionDay` in OpenAI Admin API and Mistral token-cost projections so the Today rows use the latest matching model breakdown.
- Localize the environmental section header and complete the new strings across all app language catalogs.
- Keep strict nil propagation: unsupported provider/model combinations produce no environmental rows.
- Document the methodology as a draft that still requires maintainer approval.

## Current-branch runtime proof

The app was packaged and run from code commit [`01a3ff2f`](https://github.com/dailytrap/CodexBar/commit/01a3ff2f66d5529260255cc625bcbd708d080d08). Proof-only commit [`dface252`](https://github.com/dailytrap/CodexBar/commit/dface252) adds the two redacted captures below.

### Supported model

The synthetic one-day `openai/gpt-4` fixture contains 15M tokens. Today and Last 30 days intentionally have identical values, proving duplicate-looking rows render independently with stable identities. All four environmental rows are visible without truncation.

![Supported-model provider detail showing four environmental rows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dailytrap/CodexBar/dface252/docs/environmental-impact-proof.png)

### Unsupported model

The otherwise identical `openai/gpt-5.4-unsupported-proof` fixture still shows both 15M-token cost rows, while the complete Eco section is suppressed.

![Unsupported-model provider detail showing cost rows with no environmental rows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dailytrap/CodexBar/dface252/docs/environmental-impact-unsupported-proof.png)

## Validation

- `make check`: passed; SwiftFormat reported 0/1166 files requiring formatting and SwiftLint reported 0 violations in 1165 files.
- `swift test --filter 'EnvironmentalImpactTests|MenuCardModelTests|OpenAIAPIUsageFetcherTests|MistralUsageParserTests|StatusMenuCostMenuCardTests|LocalizationLanguageCatalogTests'`: 104 tests in 20 suites passed.
- `CODEXBAR_ALLOW_LLDB=1 CODEXBAR_SIGNING=adhoc ./Scripts/package_app.sh debug`: packaged the proof build successfully from `01a3ff2f` in a clean detached worktree.
- A full-suite run exposed existing load-sensitive timing failures; all seven affected timing suites passed when rerun individually (82 tests total). No failure remained in the changed environmental, projection, menu, or localization paths.

## Remaining gate

Human product/methodology approval is still required before enabling this feature in release builds. The branch deliberately does not attempt to manufacture that approval.
target.author: dailytrap
